pullRequest.title: unify hovered_window calculation logic
pullRequest.body:
X11's logic seems flawed when the window straddles two monitors. Rather than try and fix this up, let's just do the same thing wayland does: keep track of the last window we entered, because that's the one the mouse is currently over.

Since we're doing the same thing wayland is, we can do it on the core object, and get rid of some backend specific code for x11 here.

This hopefully:

Fixes #1413

...I'm making this PR just because I don't want to lose the change. I think it's a nice improvement, but I haven't been able to verify yet, nor has anyone on that bug.

pullRequest.title: wayland: keep keybind layout constant
pullRequest.body: Fixes #4259

pullRequest.title: Plasma - client restoration fix
pullRequest.body:
The Plasma layout tries to restore clients to their original position when they're returned to the layout (e.g. from floating back to tiled). However, there is a bug where this restoration happens after any sibling clients in the same node have been removed. The layout tries to resize the sibling too and crashes because it can't find it.

Fixes #4915

pullRequest.title: improve udev rules
pullRequest.body:
...and drop `qtile udev`. It turns out that some distros (fedora, arch) run their udev daemon in the initramfs (which makes sense, of course, not sure why I didn't think of it earlier). That means that in order to run this code, we'd need to include it in the initramfs, which seems like a pain.

Instead, let's just rearrange the rules to chmod o+w things. This is simpler for users than having a special group, since groups that exists across distros are hard (sudo on ubuntu instead of  wheel on fedora, etc.). Distro packagers can use whatever rules make sense for their distro.

In the rules themselves, the backlight and battery there is just one file and it seems to exist sysfs symlink time for the device, so we can use a simple rule.

For the battery charge control, there is two files, so we need to run two commands (we could use shell globbing here to do one command, but the quoting is... annoying :).

I tried several different ways to match it at sysfs symlink creation time, but kept getting:

    BAT0: Process '/usr/bin/chmod o+w /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/charge_control_start_threshold' failed with exit code 1

because the file is only created when the specific driver is loaded. So, we trigger on that, and hard code the battery path.

All this is to say: I believe this is simpler and easier to use, since it doesn't require anything in the initramfs or having a specific group hard coded, even if it is a bit uglier.

Fixes #5227

pullRequest.title: try to always focus the previous window for an uninterrupted workflow
pullRequest.body: Solves https://github.com/qtile/qtile/issues/5264

pullRequest.title: Fix vertical clock test
pullRequest.body: Fixes #5283

pullRequest.title: rework logging path handling
pullRequest.body:
1. do not unconditionally mkdir the default log path, even if people have specified a different one
2. do allow people to specify a path that doesn't exist, we can just create the directories for them

Fixes #5286

pullRequest.title: docs: move to recommending `uv`
pullRequest.body:
I have been using uv for a while, and it's great. It is much cleaner than managing venvs, passing --break-system-packages, or whatever other hacks people have been doing. We should recommend people use it.

Fixes #5294

pullRequest.title: x11: don't ignore powered off monitors
pullRequest.body:
we recently switched to defaulting to xrandr backends, which now correctly identifies when some attached monitors are powered off, vs xinerama which did not. However, some of our bar drawing logic does not correctly reason about when to re-draw the bar when this happens, resulting in visual bugs (and missing bars).

Let's revert to the same behavior as xinerama for the time being, until we find and fix the visual bar bugs.

Fixes #5303

pullRequest.title: screensplit: fix warning spam
pullRequest.body:
See comment for details.

Fixes #5312

pullRequest.title: Wayland: Fix xwayland popups disappearing
pullRequest.body:
For override-redirect/popup surfaces, skip the normal focus path and simply give the surface keyboard focus

The normal focus path deactivates the previous surface, in this case the parent, which was probably causing the popup to also unmap

Fixes #5317

pullRequest.title: drop DefaultEventLoopPolicy
pullRequest.body:
python3.14 depreciates event loop policies entirely, in favor of just manually specifying the event loop as an argument to asyncio.run().

we were really using the event loop policy as a hack for not having a global variable, so let's switch to a global variable instead, and use it in the one place we need it.

we have to drop two verisons of python because they do not have the loop_factory= argument to asyncio:

    raise AssertionError(f"Error launching qtile, traceback:\n{error}")
    AssertionError: Error launching qtile, traceback:
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/home/runner/work/qtile/qtile/test/helpers.py", line 207, in run_qtile
        ).loop()
      File "/home/runner/work/qtile/qtile/libqtile/core/manager.py", line 216, in loop
        asyncio.run(self.async_loop(), loop_factory=lambda: libqtile.event_loop)
    TypeError: run() got an unexpected keyword argument 'loop_factory'

but maybe that's ok: 3.10 is outside of our last-three-versions policy, and 3.11 will be outside it in a few months when 3.14 is released.

There is another option here: since the motivation for this was really #2620, we could "just" switch the battery to running in-loop, which would mean that it is not run via run_in_executor() i.e. in a separate thread. That means that get_running_loop() would return the right loop, and we wouldn't need this hack at all.

(It also means that the battery could potentially block the UI while reading from e.g. procfs/sysfs files, but that seems unlikely. Perhaps it also suggests another widget type: something with an `async def poll()`, which could run in the main loop and avoid both of these problems.)

The only other widget which uses send_notification() is pomodoro, which doesn't read any files at all and just uses gettimeofday(), which is a VDSO i.e. memory access, that should also not block, so if we convert the battery widget, we should do this one as well.

Finally, we suggest use of send_notification() in the hooks documentation, but all hooks are fired from the main thread, so the get_running_loop() call should work fine there.

That does mean we'd have to be careful to always send_notification() from the main thread going forward, but it doesn't seem like that hard of a challenge.

Fixes #5346

pullRequest.title: bar: preserve bar and widget references during finalization
pullRequest.body:
The core insight here is: finalization is for the resources allocated by a bar or widget, not deleting the bar or widgets themselves. If we do that, then when people turn their monitors back on, we will have mutilated their configs, deleting the bars.

Instead, we should finalize these python objects, since they know how to re-allocate the resources as necessary on _configure() again if someone re-attaches a screen. However, we do not do silly things like widgets.clear() (deleting all the widgets from a bar), or screen.top = None, removing the widget from the Screen.

This lets us revert 99c8a54b026d ("x11: don't ignore powered off monitors"), since we really do want to ignore powered off monitors, but we couldn't before because we were deleting widgets and bars from configs when the monitors were disconnected.

Fixes #5436

pullRequest.title: wayland: Update current screen on cursor motion
pullRequest.body:
Ensure the compositor updates the current output when the cursor moves, so that layer-shell views and XDG popups appear on the correct screen

Fixes #5511, #5641

pullRequest.title: Wayland - add is_transient_for support
pullRequest.body: Closes #5574

pullRequest.title: Fix stdio redirection to /dev/null
pullRequest.body: Closes #5595

pullRequest.title: wayland: Give server key bindings precedence
pullRequest.body:
Server key bindings are processed first and suppress internal window bindings when matched, consistent with the behaviour of the old backend

Fixes #5605

pullRequest.title: [wayland] Fix xwayland crashes on menus/popups
pullRequest.body:
Caused by incorrect listener fields in multiple wl_container_of() calls

Would like to take the credit, but GPT spotted the first one

Fixes #5616

pullRequest.title: [wayland] Notify pointer motion when dragging
pullRequest.body:
When dragging in firefox, pointer focus is lost, so we must also notify pointer motion while dragging

Fixes #5619

pullRequest.title: wayland: Fix segfault when cairo surface freed
pullRequest.body:
In #5639, we are crashing in Drawer._draw() when trying to get cairo surface from pointer - it appears the surface reference can be a dangling pointer if the view has been killed and surface destroyed

This can be prevented by NULLing the pointer after destroying the surface

Fixes #5639

pullRequest.title: Docs cleanups
pullRequest.body: Hopefully fixes #5653?

pullRequest.title: wayland: Update XDG surfaces if geometry changed
pullRequest.body:
Some clients update their surface geometry after being mapped. When this occurs we should reposition the surface

Sway and old backend do/did something similar

Fixes #5654

pullRequest.title: [wayland] add idle notify/inhibit support
pullRequest.body:
Adds support for wayland's idle-notify and idle-inhibit extensions.

Inhibitors can be created in a number of ways:
  - By the application itself
  - In users' configs (via a new `wl_idle_inhibitors` variable)
  - Dynamically (`lazy.window.add_idle_inhibitor()`)
  - Globally (`lazy.core.set_idle_inhibitor()`)

Inhibitors can be set to be active when a window is open, focused, visible or fullscreen.


This is draft for now as I need people to test it.

Known issues (to be updated as people report bugs):
- [ ] ~fd errors when exiting qtile (not 100% certain that's linked to this PR...)~ Not this PR.
- [x] formatting, mypy checks etc
- [x] missing tests
- [ ] missing documentation (could be in a later PR)
- [ ] add a widget to show inhibitor status

pullRequest.title: [wayland] Implement pointer constraints
pullRequest.body:
Pointer can be locked or confined to a region. Relative motion events are emitted when locked

Closely follows how this is implemented in sway

Limited testing with https://stackoverflow.com/a/78838558

Additional real world testing with the following games working correctly:
- Helldivers 2
- Satisfactory  
- RV There Yet
- System Shock (2023)
- Elden Ring
- Doom Eternal
- Counter Strike 2
- Batman Arkham Knight

Fixes #5668

pullRequest.title: wayland: Add core.get_mouse_position()
pullRequest.body: Fixes #5669

pullRequest.title: wayland: Move float above when floats_kept_above=True
pullRequest.body:
Mirroring the same checks in the x11 backend

Fixes #5688

pullRequest.title: wayland: Don't mark non-internal windows killed
pullRequest.body:
Clients may ignore a close request on a parent toplevel when it has an
open child. Since we can't detect success when closing a window, avoid
marking the window as killed, because this will prevent the parent from
being closed later

We will still track killed state for internal windows, which don't have
this issue, to prevent use after free

Fixes #5689

pullRequest.title: tests: switch to pytest-httpbin
pullRequest.body:
This will let everyone not have to deal with the httpbin service being flaky, not just github actions.

Fixes #5691

pullRequest.title: wayland: Fix keyboard repeat failing to update
pullRequest.body:
Move callback that updates repeat rate/delay to after input is fully initialised

Fixes #5695

pullRequest.title: [wayland] convert keycodes to keysyms
pullRequest.body: Fixes #5700

pullRequest.title: wayland: Add xcursor configuration
pullRequest.body:
Set xcursor size and theme from config variables: wl_xcursor_size and
wl_xcursor_theme

Added a general function for reading parameters from qtile config,
for this purpose

Largely following sway's implementation

Fixes #5706

Tested (as best I can):
- [x] Setting base cursor size
- [x] Cursor scales automatically with output scale
- [x] Size updates when changed in config and config reloaded
- [x] Setting cursor theme

pullRequest.title: wayland: Fix handling repeats for key bindings
pullRequest.body:
Bug: Key bindings not repeating

Fixes #5707

pullRequest.title: wayland: Fix pointer constraints focus and warp
pullRequest.body:
When we constrain the cursor, it's possible the constrained surface hasn't received pointer focus yet, even if has been placed over the cursor. So check for pointer focus and update as required. (And if we're not over the surface we already warp and focus). Also ensure constraints are updated when we change focus

Fix incorrect coordinate transforms affecting warping

Fix implicit grab behaviour when using pointer constraints

Fixes #5718 and #5757

pullRequest.title: wayland: plasma layout check payload when removing window
pullRequest.body:
When a plasma window is made floating, it is removed from the plasma layout. When this window is killed, qtile.unmanage() will call the layouts's remove function, but since the window has already been removed, it ends up calling remove on None

This issue manifests on wayland when the floating window has been fullscreened, because the fullscreen background is subsequently not removed

Fix by checking for None before calling remove

Fixes #5734

pullRequest.title: Net widget: fix crash on missing interface
pullRequest.body:
Net widget previously crashed if an inteface was unavailable. We now handle missing interface with a configurable option to warn user that the interace is not found.

Fixes #5737

pullRequest.title: Fix PulseVolume missing callbacks
pullRequest.body:
When we refactored the volume widgets, PulseVolume no longer inherited the `Volume` widget which meant it lost the mouse callbacks that were defined in that widget.

Fixes #5747

pullRequest.title: Fix groupbox crash when padding is None
pullRequest.body:
The GroupBox widget inherits from `base._TextBox` and, as a result, the docs explain that setting a `padding` value of `None` will cause the widget to calculate the padding. However, `GroupBox._configure` does not call `base._TextBox._configure` and so the calculation is never performed. This can result in a crash as the widget tries to calculate positions by including the padding value, resulting in a `TypeError`.

Fixes #5752

pullRequest.title: Don't finalise widgets twice
pullRequest.body:
`Qtile._finalize_configurables` finalises every widget in `widgets_map`. It then finalises every `Bar` and `Gap` instance. `Bar.finalize` will try to finalise every widget in the bar. This can result in log noise where widgets have subscribed to hooks and tries to unsubscribe having already unsubscribed.

Widgets have a `finalized` flag so let's just check the widget hasn't been finalised before calling `finalize()`.

Fixes #5753

pullRequest.title: wayland: Update pointer focus when view under pointer changes
pullRequest.body:
Currently, in most circumstances, pointer focus only changes when the
pointer is moved

This commit adds checks to update pointer focus when views are
created, moved/resized and destroyed

Fixes #5775

Also cleaned up some code in xwayland's commit function while it was staring me in the face

pullRequest.title: widget: move GenPollUrl to its own file
pullRequest.body:
The lazy_import() machinery catches all ImportErrors and logs an error if people try to use a particular widget but don't have the dependencies. As a result, we don't need this try/catch ImportError for aiohttp.

However, since there are several widgets that live in this file, and all of them will fail to load if aiohttp is not installed, it's best to move GenPollUrl to its own file, so that only GenPollUrl is unavailable if aiohttp is missing.

Fixes #5785

pullRequest.title: wayland: Implement warp pointer with focus
pullRequest.body: Fixes #5796

pullRequest.title: wayland: Fix output enable+position crash
pullRequest.body:
Ensure output is committed before adding to layout

Fixes #5807

pullRequest.title: wayland: Fix cursor_warp focus loop
pullRequest.body:
In certain circumstances, appears to be a loop between: focus warp
cursor -> process_motion cursor_motion_cb -> _focus_pointer calling
focus -> focus warp cursor

Break the loop by updating pointer focus after warp without processing
motion

Add a warp with motion option for fake_motion()

Fixes #5841

pullRequest.title: Fix window lost focus when dragged between screens
pullRequest.body:
Floating windows lose their focus border when dragged between screens. This is because the floating layout draws the border if client.has_focus is True. This is the case if the window is equal to qtile.current_window. qtile.current_window looks at the active group on the current screen. However, when dragging windows, we only change screen focus after the window has been added to the other group so qtile.current_window is looking at the old screen.

Fixes #5843

pullRequest.title: Expose command to reset idle timers
pullRequest.body:
Adds a new `idle_notify_activity` command which has the effect of resetting idle timers. This is useful for people scripting who want to reset timers without user input.

On wayland, we call `qw_server_idle_notify_activity` while, on x11, we send a fake motion event.

Closes #5847

pullRequest.title: [wayland] Fix build if no xwayland support
pullRequest.body:
Qtile would not compile if wlroots was built without xwayland support as our build script resulted in references to xwayland specific code and because cffi doesn't preprocess headers.

Fixes #5884

pullRequest.title: Fix Volume widget crash on reload
pullRequest.body:
The Volume widget has a background task to check for volume changes. This task is not cleared when the config is reloaded and so tries to draw the bar with references to finalised `Drawer` and `TextLayout` objects.

This PR ensures the task is deleted when the widget is finalised.

Fixes #5886

pullRequest.title: Only pass button presses to bar when press inside bar
pullRequest.body:
On wayland, the bar can still receive button press events, even when the mouse is not over the bar. This PR doesn't address that underlying issue but adds a check in `bar.py` to only forward button presses to widgets when the coordinates are inside the bar (the code previously only checked one axis).

Fixes #5892

pullRequest.title: Initialise fontconfig before starting qtile
pullRequest.body:
Prevent unsynchronised initialisation calls by initialising before main qtile code

Attempting to fix #5896

An experiment - have had some success on my local branch

AI says because it is called with subprocess it runs in a separate process and the only benefit is it may warm up the cache

Ideally then, we should try calling FcInit directly, which would require a cffi reference to libfontconfig

pullRequest.title: wayland: Disable client focus when exclusive_layer held
pullRequest.body: Fixes #5901

pullRequest.title: Allow WindowName to have calculated width
pullRequest.body:
By default, the `WindowName` widget is a `STRETCH` widget which expands to fill available space. That is not ideal if you want the widget to be centred between two `Spacer` widgets as they will all take an equal width.

This PR adds a `stretch` configuration option which, when set to `False`, will ensure the widget's length matches its contents.

Fixes #5903
